label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about how the device works. The passage says that the Dutch artist, Daan Roosegaarde, has a device that uses an electromagnetic field to pull particles in the smog to the ground. So the main idea is that the electromagnetic field is what's doing the pulling. Looking at the choices: A) helping keep the particles out with more masks. But the article mentions that the device is intended to get rid of pollutants so people don't need masks. The masks are part of the problem, not the solution here. So A is probably not right. B) pulling pollutants to the ground with an electromagnetic field. This directly matches what the article says. The device uses an electromagnetic field to pull particles down. C) absorbing hair with a balloon with static. The article uses the balloon and hair analogy to explain the electromagnetic field's effect, but that's just an example, not how the actual device works. The device isn't using a balloon; it's using an electromagnetic field. So C is more of
